# Generated by ContextEngine — npx @strifero/contextengine
# https://github.com/strifero/ContextEngine

# Project Context

## Stack
- NestJS
- TypeScript (strict mode)
- TypeORM or Prisma (detected from deps)
- PostgreSQL
- Jest for testing

## Architecture
- `src/modules/` — Feature modules (each is a NestJS module)
- `src/common/` — Shared decorators, guards, pipes, interceptors
- `src/config/` — Configuration service and validation
- Each module contains: controller, service, entity/dto, module definition

## Conventions

### Modules
- One module per feature domain
- Keep the module boundary clean — services don't reach across modules directly
- Use dependency injection for everything — avoid `new` for services

### Controllers
- Controllers handle HTTP routing and request/response shaping only
- Delegate all business logic to services
- Use DTOs with class-validator decorators for request validation
- Use `@ApiTags` and `@ApiOperation` decorators (Swagger)

### Services
- Services hold business logic and data access
- Services are injectable singletons — design them to be stateless
- Use TypeORM repositories or Prisma client injected via constructor

### DTOs
- One DTO per operation (CreateUserDto, UpdateUserDto — not a shared UserDto)
- Use `class-validator` decorators for all validation
- Use `class-transformer` for type coercion from request bodies

### Error handling
- Use NestJS built-in exception filters (`HttpException`, `NotFoundException`, etc.)
- Create custom exception classes for domain errors
- Never let database errors bubble up to HTTP responses raw

## What to avoid
- Do not put business logic in controllers
- Do not use `@Inject()` with string tokens — use typed injection tokens
- Do not bypass the module system with circular imports
